channel.title: SRE Blog
channel.description: Recent blog posts on SRE Blog

item.title: Reasons to write in English
item.description: I started this blog a long time ago and of course, I started to write it in my native language. I did not even think I would work with colleagues, not from my country. As I more and more work with international companies I decided to switch my blog to a bilingual model. The plan is simple: New articles would be published in both languages Old articles written in Russian would stay as it is Later I may translate some of them if they are not completely obsolete To keep data accessible I create rewrite rules so Google (hopefully) will not downrate my SERP position Not sure how it would work but let&rsquo;s see.

item.title: Site and author
item.description: My name is Paul Rudnitskiy and this is my personal blog. Computers always fascinating me, their flexibility and complexity, the things you can achieve with those cold lifeless pieces of silicon. I started to work as a computer technician in 2001, first as a system administrator but tried many infrastructure-related roles &ndash; DC technician, system administrator, network engineer, and even a part-time programmer. Later I switched to DevOps and nowadays I&rsquo;m doing SRE and tech lead roles. I mostly work with Unix systems, Debian Linux is the most common OS I work with. I do not work with Microsoft products and Microsoft Windows specifically. You can find my full professional biography on LinkedIN.
